138: class DeliveryTracker:
139:     def __init__(self):
140:         self.pending_messages: Dict[str, Tuple[str, float, bool]] = {}
141:         self.sent_acks: Set[str] = set()
142:     
143:     def track_message(self, message_id: str, content: str, is_private: bool):
144:         self.pending_messages[message_id] = (content, time.time(), is_private)
145:     
146:     def mark_delivered(self, message_id: str) -> bool:
147:         return self.pending_messages.pop(message_id, None) is not None
148:     
149:     def should_send_ack(self, ack_id: str) -> bool:
150:         if ack_id in self.sent_acks:
151:             return False
152:         self.sent_acks.add(ack_id)
153:         return True

155: class FragmentCollector:
156:     def __init__(self):
157:         self.fragments: Dict[str, Dict[int, bytes]] = {}
158:         self.metadata: Dict[str, Tuple[int, int, str]] = {}
159:     
160:     def add_fragment(self, fragment_id: bytes, index: int, total: int, 
161:                     original_type: int, data: bytes, sender_id: str) -> Optional[Tuple[bytes, str]]:
162:         fragment_id_hex = fragment_id.hex()
163:         
164:         debug_full_println(f"[COLLECTOR] Adding fragment {index + 1}/{total} for ID {fragment_id_hex[:8]}")
165:         
166:         if fragment_id_hex not in self.fragments:
167:             debug_full_println(f"[COLLECTOR] Creating new fragment collection for ID {fragment_id_hex[:8]}")
168:             self.fragments[fragment_id_hex] = {}
169:             self.metadata[fragment_id_hex] = (total, original_type, sender_id)
170:         
171:         fragment_map = self.fragments[fragment_id_hex]
172:         fragment_map[index] = data
173:         debug_full_println(f"[COLLECTOR] Fragment {index + 1} stored. Have {len(fragment_map)}/{total} fragments")
174:         
175:         if len(fragment_map) == total:
176:             debug_full_println("[COLLECTOR] ✓ All fragments received! Reassembling...")
177:             
178:             complete_data = bytearray()
179:             for i in range(total):
180:                 if i in fragment_map:
181:                     debug_full_println(f"[COLLECTOR] Appending fragment {i + 1} ({len(fragment_map[i])} bytes)")
182:                     complete_data.extend(fragment_map[i])
183:                 else:
184:                     debug_full_println(f"[COLLECTOR] ✗ Missing fragment {i + 1}")
185:                     return None
186:             
187:             debug_full_println(f"[COLLECTOR] ✓ Reassembly complete: {len(complete_data)} bytes total")
188:             
189:             sender = self.metadata.get(fragment_id_hex, (0, 0, "Unknown"))[2]
190:             
191:             del self.fragments[fragment_id_hex]
192:             del self.metadata[fragment_id_hex]
193:             
194:             return (bytes(complete_data), sender)
195:         
196:         return None
197:

233:     def _on_peer_authenticated(self, peer_id: str, fingerprint: str):

240:     def _on_handshake_required(self, peer_id: str):
241:         """Callback when handshake is required for a peer"""
242:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake required for peer {peer_id}")
243:         # The handshake will be initiated when trying to send private messages
244:     
245:     async def send_pending_private_messages(self, peer_id: str):
246:         """Send all pending private messages for a peer after handshake completes"""
247:         if peer_id not in self.pending_private_messages:
248:             return
249:         
250:         pending_messages = self.pending_private_messages.pop(peer_id, [])
251:         if not pending_messages:

253:         
254:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Sending {len(pending_messages)} pending messages to {peer_id}")
255:         
256:         for content, nickname, message_id in pending_messages:
257:             try:
258:                 # Add longer delay before sending to allow BLE queue to clear
259:                 await asyncio.sleep(0.3)
260:                 # Call the actual send function with established session
261:                 await self.send_private_message(content, peer_id, nickname, message_id)
262:                 # Small delay between messages
263:                 await asyncio.sleep(0.2)
264:             except Exception as e:

269:                     if peer_id not in self.pending_private_messages:

384:     async def handshake(self):
385:         """Perform initial handshake"""

387:

398:                 timestamp_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)

400:                 signing_public_key_bytes = self.encryption_service.get_signing_public_key_bytes()

402:                 # Create binding data for signature (matching iOS)
403:                 # iOS uses: peerID + publicKey + timestamp (as string)

405:                 binding_data = self.my_peer_id.encode('utf-8') + public_key_bytes + timestamp_data
406:                 signature = self.encryption_service.sign_data(binding_data)

409:                 identity_payload = self.encode_noise_identity_announcement_binary(

411:                     self.nickname, timestamp_ms, signature

416:                 )

423:                 # Fallback to old key exchange
424:                 handshake_message = self.encryption_service.initiate_handshake(self.my_peer_id)
425:                 handshake_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
426:                     self.my_peer_id, MessageType.KEY_EXCHANGE, handshake_message
427:                 )
428:                 await self.send_packet(handshake_packet)

436:             )
437:             await self.send_packet(announce_packet)
438:             
439:             debug_println("[3] Handshake sent. You can now chat.")
440:         else:

442:             print("\033[90m» Running in offline mode. Waiting for peers...\033[0m")
443:         
444:         if self.app_state.nickname:

450:         self.channel_creators = self.app_state.channel_creators
451:         self.password_protected_channels = self.app_state.password_protected_channels
452:         self.channel_key_commitments = self.app_state.channel_key_commitments

465:     async def send_packet(self, packet: bytes):
466:         """Send packet, with fragmentation if needed"""
467:         debug_full_println(f"[RAW SEND] {packet.hex()}")
468:         if not self.client or not self.characteristic:
469:             debug_println("[!] No connection available. Message queued.")
470:             # In a real implementation, we might queue messages here
471:             return
472:         
473:         # Check if still connected before sending
474:         if not self.client.is_connected:
475:             debug_println("[!] Connection lost. Cannot send packet.")
476:             # Trigger disconnection handling if not already done
477:             if self.client:
478:                 self.handle_disconnect(self.client)
479:             return
480:             
481:         if should_fragment(packet):

483:         else:
484:             write_with_response = len(packet) > 512

486:                 # Add small delay to prevent blocking errors

489:                     self.characteristic,

491:                     response=write_with_response
492:                 )
493:             except Exception as e:

497:                     if self.client:

499:                     return
500:                 
501:                 # Handle blocking errors by retrying without response
502:                 if "could not complete without blocking" in str(e) or write_with_response:

515:                             if self.client:

517:                         elif "could not complete without blocking" in str(e2):

525:     async def send_packet_with_fragmentation(self, packet: bytes):
526:         """Fragment and send large packets"""
527:         if not self.client or not self.characteristic:
528:             debug_println("[!] No connection available. Cannot send fragmented message.")
529:             return
530:         
531:         # Check if still connected
532:         if not self.client.is_connected:
533:             debug_println("[!] Connection lost. Cannot send fragmented packet.")
534:             if self.client:
535:                 self.handle_disconnect(self.client)
536:             return
537:             
538:         debug_println(f"[FRAG] Original packet size: {len(packet)} bytes")
539:         
540:         fragment_size = 150  # Conservative size for iOS BLE
541:         chunks = [packet[i:i+fragment_size] for i in range(0, len(packet), fragment_size)]
542:         total_fragments = len(chunks)
543:         
544:         fragment_id = os.urandom(8)
545:         debug_println(f"[FRAG] Fragment ID: {fragment_id.hex()}")
546:         debug_println(f"[FRAG] Total fragments: {total_fragments}")
547:         
548:         for index, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
549:             if index == 0:
550:                 fragment_type = MessageType.FRAGMENT_START
551:             elif index == len(chunks) - 1:
552:                 fragment_type = MessageType.FRAGMENT_END
553:             else:
554:                 fragment_type = MessageType.FRAGMENT_CONTINUE
555:             
556:             # Create fragment payload
557:             fragment_payload = bytearray()
558:             fragment_payload.extend(fragment_id)
559:             fragment_payload.extend(struct.pack('>H', index))
560:             fragment_payload.extend(struct.pack('>H', total_fragments))
561:             fragment_payload.append(MessageType.MESSAGE.value)
562:             fragment_payload.extend(chunk)
563:             
564:             fragment_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
565:                 self.my_peer_id,
566:                 fragment_type,
567:                 bytes(fragment_payload)
568:             )
569:             
570:             try:
571:                 await self.client.write_gatt_char(
572:                     self.characteristic,
573:                     fragment_packet,
574:                     response=False
575:                 )
576:                 
577:                 debug_println(f"[FRAG] ✓ Fragment {index + 1}/{total_fragments} sent")
578:                 
579:                 if index < len(chunks) - 1:
580:                     await asyncio.sleep(0.02)  # 20ms delay
581:             except Exception as e:
582:                 if "not connected" in str(e).lower():
583:                     debug_println(f"[FRAG] Connection lost while sending fragment {index + 1}")
584:                     if self.client:
585:                         self.handle_disconnect(self.client)
586:                     return
587:                 else:
588:                     raise e
589:     
590:     async def notification_handler(self, sender: BleakGATTCharacteristic, data: bytes):
591:         """Handle incoming BLE notifications"""
592:         try:
593:             # Enhanced hex logging to match iOS format
594:             hex_string = ' '.join(f'{b:02X}' for b in data)
595:             debug_full_println(f"[RAW RECV] Received {len(data)} bytes")
596:             debug_full_println(f"[RAW RECV] {hex_string}")
597:         except BlockingIOError:
598:             # If even debug printing fails due to blocking, just silently continue
599:             pass
600:             
601:         try:
602:             packet = parse_bitchat_packet(data)
603:             
604:             # Ignore our own messages (they are already displayed when sent)
605:             if packet.sender_id_str == self.my_peer_id:
606:                 return
607:             
608:             await self.handle_packet(packet, data)
609:             
610:         except Exception as e:
611:             try:
612:                 debug_full_println(f"[ERROR] Failed to parse packet: {e}")
613:             except BlockingIOError:
614:                 # Silently ignore blocking errors
615:                 pass
616:     
617:     async def handle_packet(self, packet: BitchatPacket, raw_data: bytes):
618:         """Handle incoming packet"""
619:         if packet.msg_type == MessageType.ANNOUNCE:
620:             await self.handle_announce(packet)
621:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.MESSAGE:
622:             await self.handle_message(packet, raw_data)
623:         elif packet.msg_type in [MessageType.FRAGMENT_START, MessageType.FRAGMENT_CONTINUE, MessageType.FRAGMENT_END]:
624:             await self.handle_fragment(packet, raw_data)
625:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.KEY_EXCHANGE:
626:             await self.handle_key_exchange(packet)
627:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.NOISE_HANDSHAKE_INIT:
628:             await self.handle_noise_handshake_init(packet)
629:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.NOISE_HANDSHAKE_RESP:
630:             await self.handle_noise_handshake_resp(packet)
631:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.NOISE_ENCRYPTED:
632:             await self.handle_noise_encrypted(packet, raw_data)
633:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.LEAVE:
634:             await self.handle_leave(packet)
635:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.CHANNEL_ANNOUNCE:
636:             await self.handle_channel_announce(packet)
637:         elif packet.msg_type == MessageType.NOISE_IDENTITY_ANNOUNCE:
638:             await self.handle_noise_identity_announce(packet)
639:     
640:     async def handle_announce(self, packet: BitchatPacket):
641:         """Handle peer announcement"""
642:         peer_nickname = packet.payload.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore').strip()
643:         is_new_peer = packet.sender_id_str not in self.peers
644:         
645:         if packet.sender_id_str not in self.peers:
646:             self.peers[packet.sender_id_str] = Peer()
647:         
648:         self.peers[packet.sender_id_str].nickname = peer_nickname
649:         
650:         if is_new_peer:
651:             print(f"\r\033[K\033[33m{peer_nickname} connected\033[0m\n> ", end='', flush=True)
652:             debug_println(f"[<-- RECV] Announce: Peer {packet.sender_id_str} is now known as '{peer_nickname}'")
653:             
654:             # Apply tie-breaker logic like iOS client
655:             if self.my_peer_id < packet.sender_id_str:
656:                 # We have lower ID, initiate handshake
657:                 debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Initiating Noise handshake with new peer {packet.sender_id_str} (tie-breaker: we have lower ID)")
658:                 try:
659:                     handshake_message = self.encryption_service.initiate_handshake(packet.sender_id_str)
660:                     handshake_packet = create_bitchat_packet_with_recipient(
661:                         self.my_peer_id, packet.sender_id_str, MessageType.NOISE_HANDSHAKE_INIT, handshake_message, None
662:                     )
663:                     # Set TTL to 3 like iOS
664:                     handshake_data = bytearray(handshake_packet)
665:                     handshake_data[2] = 3
666:                     handshake_packet = bytes(handshake_data)
667:                     await self.send_packet(handshake_packet)
668:                     debug_println(f"[NOISE] Sent handshake init to {packet.sender_id_str}, payload size: {len(handshake_message)}")
669:                 except Exception as e:
670:                     debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Failed to initiate handshake: {e}")
671:                     # Fallback to old key exchange
672:                     key_exchange_payload = self.encryption_service.get_combined_public_key_data()
673:                     key_exchange_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
674:                         self.my_peer_id, MessageType.KEY_EXCHANGE, key_exchange_payload
675:                     )
676:                     await self.send_packet(key_exchange_packet)
677:             else:
678:                 # We have higher ID, send targeted identity announce to prompt them to initiate
679:                 debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Sending targeted identity announce to {packet.sender_id_str} (tie-breaker: they have lower ID)")
680:                 try:
681:                     timestamp_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
682:                     public_key_bytes = self.encryption_service.get_public_key()
683:                     signing_public_key_bytes = self.encryption_service.get_signing_public_key_bytes()
684:                     
685:                     # Create binding data for signature
686:                     timestamp_data = str(timestamp_ms).encode('utf-8')
687:                     binding_data = self.my_peer_id.encode('utf-8') + public_key_bytes + timestamp_data
688:                     signature = self.encryption_service.sign_data(binding_data)
689:                     
690:                     # Encode to binary format
691:                     identity_payload = self.encode_noise_identity_announcement_binary(
692:                         self.my_peer_id, public_key_bytes, signing_public_key_bytes,
693:                         self.nickname, timestamp_ms, signature
694:                     )
695:                     
696:                     identity_packet = create_bitchat_packet_with_recipient(
697:                         self.my_peer_id, packet.sender_id_str, MessageType.NOISE_IDENTITY_ANNOUNCE, 
698:                         identity_payload, signature
699:                     )
700:                     await self.send_packet(identity_packet)
701:                 except Exception as e:
702:                     debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Failed to send targeted identity announce: {e}")
703:     
704:     async def handle_message(self, packet: BitchatPacket, raw_data: bytes):
705:         """Handle chat message"""
706:         # Check if sender is blocked
707:         fingerprint = self.encryption_service.get_peer_fingerprint(packet.sender_id_str)
708:         if fingerprint and fingerprint in self.blocked_peers:
709:             debug_println(f"[BLOCKED] Ignoring message from blocked peer: {packet.sender_id_str}")
710:             return
711:         
712:         # Check if message is for us
713:         is_broadcast = packet.recipient_id == BROADCAST_RECIPIENT if packet.recipient_id else True
714:         is_for_us = is_broadcast or (packet.recipient_id_str == self.my_peer_id)
715:         
716:         if not is_for_us:
717:             # Relay if TTL > 1
718:             if packet.ttl > 1:
719:                 await asyncio.sleep(random.uniform(0.01, 0.05))
720:                 relay_data = bytearray(raw_data)
721:                 relay_data[2] = packet.ttl - 1
722:                 await self.send_packet(bytes(relay_data))
723:             return
724:         is_private_message = not is_broadcast and is_for_us
725:         decrypted_payload = None
726:         if is_private_message:
727:             try:
728:                 decrypted_payload = self.encryption_service.decrypt_from_peer(packet.sender_id_str, packet.payload)
729:                 debug_println("[PRIVATE] Successfully decrypted private message!")
730:             except NoiseError:
731:                 debug_println("[PRIVATE] Failed to decrypt private message")
732:                 return
733:         # Parse message first to check if it's actually a private message
734:         try:
735:             if is_private_message and decrypted_payload:
736:                 unpadded = unpad_message(decrypted_payload)
737:                 message = parse_bitchat_message_payload(unpadded)
738:             else:
739:                 message = parse_bitchat_message_payload(packet.payload)
740:             # Check for duplicates using both bloom filter and set
741:             if message.id not in self.processed_messages:
742:                 # Add to bloom filter and set
743:                 self.bloom.add(message.id)
744:                 self.processed_messages.add(message.id)
745:                 
746:                 # Display the message
747:                 await self.display_message(message, packet, is_private_message)
748:                 
749:                 # Send ACK if needed
750:                 if should_send_ack(is_private_message, message.channel, None, self.nickname, len(self.peers)):
751:                     await self.send_delivery_ack(message.id, packet.sender_id_str, is_private_message)
752:                 
753:                 # Relay if TTL > 1
754:                 if packet.ttl > 1:
755:                     await asyncio.sleep(random.uniform(0.01, 0.05))
756:                     relay_data = bytearray(raw_data)
757:                     relay_data[2] = packet.ttl - 1
758:                     await self.send_packet(bytes(relay_data))
759:             else:
760:                 debug_println(f"[DUPLICATE] Ignoring duplicate message: {message.id}")
761:                     
762:         except Exception as e:
763:             debug_full_println(f"[ERROR] Failed to parse message: {e}")
764:     
765:     async def display_message(self, message: BitchatMessage, packet: BitchatPacket, is_private: bool):
766:         """Display a message in the terminal"""
767:         sender_nick = self.peers.get(packet.sender_id_str, Peer()).nickname or packet.sender_id_str
768:         
769:         # Track discovered channels
770:         if message.channel:
771:             self.discovered_channels.add(message.channel)
772:             if message.is_encrypted:
773:                 self.password_protected_channels.add(message.channel)
774:         
775:         # Decrypt channel messages if we have the key
776:         display_content = message.content
777:         if message.is_encrypted and message.channel and message.channel in self.channel_keys:
778:             try:
779:                 creator_fingerprint = self.channel_creators.get(message.channel, '')
780:                 decrypted = self.encryption_service.decrypt_from_channel(
781:                     message.encrypted_content,
782:                     message.channel,
783:                     self.channel_keys[message.channel],
784:                     creator_fingerprint
785:                 )
786:                 display_content = decrypted
787:             except:
788:                 display_content = "[Encrypted message - decryption failed]"
789:         elif message.is_encrypted:
790:             display_content = "[Encrypted message - join channel with password]"
791:         
792:         # Check for cover traffic
793:         if is_private and display_content.startswith(COVER_TRAFFIC_PREFIX):
794:             debug_println(f"[COVER] Discarding dummy message from {sender_nick}")
795:             return
796:         
797:         # Update chat context for private messages
798:         if is_private:
799:             self.chat_context.last_private_sender = (packet.sender_id_str, sender_nick)
800:             self.chat_context.add_dm(sender_nick, packet.sender_id_str)
801:         
802:         # Format and display
803:         timestamp = datetime.now()
804:         display = format_message_display(
805:             timestamp,
806:             sender_nick,
807:             display_content,
808:             is_private,
809:             bool(message.channel),
810:             message.channel,
811:             self.nickname if is_private else None,
812:             self.nickname
813:         )
814:         
815:         print(f"\r\033[K{display}")
816:         
817:         if is_private and not isinstance(self.chat_context.current_mode, PrivateDM):
818:             print("\033[90m» /reply to respond\033[0m")
819:         
820:         print("> ", end='', flush=True)

822:     async def handle_fragment(self, packet: BitchatPacket, raw_data: bytes):
823:         """Handle message fragment"""
824:         if len(packet.payload) >= 13:
825:             fragment_id = packet.payload[0:8]
826:             index = struct.unpack('>H', packet.payload[8:10])[0]
827:             total = struct.unpack('>H', packet.payload[10:12])[0]
828:             original_type = packet.payload[12]
829:             fragment_data = packet.payload[13:]
830:             
831:             result = self.fragment_collector.add_fragment(
832:                 fragment_id, index, total, original_type, fragment_data, packet.sender_id_str
833:             )
834:             
835:             if result:
836:                 complete_data, _ = result
837:                 reassembled_packet = parse_bitchat_packet(complete_data)
838:                 await self.handle_packet(reassembled_packet, complete_data)
839:         
840:         # Relay fragment if TTL > 1
841:         if packet.ttl > 1:
842:             await asyncio.sleep(random.uniform(0.01, 0.05))
843:             relay_data = bytearray(raw_data)
844:             relay_data[2] = packet.ttl - 1
845:             await self.send_packet(bytes(relay_data))
846:     
847:     async def handle_key_exchange(self, packet: BitchatPacket):
848:         """Handle key exchange"""
849:         try:
850:             # Convert bytearray to bytes for encryption service
851:             payload_bytes = bytes(packet.payload) if isinstance(packet.payload, bytearray) else packet.payload
852:             response = self.encryption_service.process_handshake_message(packet.sender_id_str, payload_bytes)
853:             if response:
854:                 response_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
855:                     self.my_peer_id, MessageType.KEY_EXCHANGE, response
856:                 )
857:                 await self.send_packet(response_packet)
858:             
859:             if self.encryption_service.is_session_established(packet.sender_id_str):
860:                 debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Handshake completed with {packet.sender_id_str}")
861:                 # If this is a new peer after reconnection, send our key exchange too
862:                 if packet.sender_id_str not in self.peers:
863:                     debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Sending key exchange response to new peer {packet.sender_id_str}")
864:                     handshake_message = self.encryption_service.initiate_handshake(packet.sender_id_str)
865:                     key_exchange_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
866:                         self.my_peer_id, MessageType.KEY_EXCHANGE, handshake_message
867:                     )
868:                     await self.send_packet(key_exchange_packet)
869: 
870:         except Exception as e:
871:             debug_println(f"[CRYPTO] Handshake failed with {packet.sender_id_str}: {e}")
872:     
873:     async def handle_noise_handshake_init(self, packet: BitchatPacket):
874:         """Handle Noise handshake initiation"""
875:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Received handshake init from {packet.sender_id_str}")
876:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Recipient ID: {packet.recipient_id_str}, My ID: {self.my_peer_id}")
877:         
878:         # Check if this handshake is for us
879:         if packet.recipient_id_str and packet.recipient_id_str != self.my_peer_id:
880:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake not for us, ignoring")
881:             return
882:             
883:         # Check payload size 
884:         payload_size = len(packet.payload)
885:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake payload size: {payload_size} bytes")
886:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake payload hex: {packet.payload.hex()[:64]}...")
887:         
888:         try:
889:             # Convert bytearray to bytes for encryption service
890:             payload_bytes = bytes(packet.payload) if isinstance(packet.payload, bytearray) else packet.payload
891:             response = self.encryption_service.process_handshake_message(packet.sender_id_str, payload_bytes)
892:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] process_handshake_message returned: {bool(response)}, response size: {len(response) if response else 0}")
893:             
894:             if response:
895:                 # Send handshake response with proper recipient
896:                 response_packet = create_bitchat_packet_with_recipient(
897:                     self.my_peer_id, packet.sender_id_str, MessageType.NOISE_HANDSHAKE_RESP, response, None
898:                 )
899:                 # Set TTL to 3 like iOS
900:                 response_data = bytearray(response_packet)
901:                 response_data[2] = 3
902:                 await self.send_packet(bytes(response_data))
903:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Sent handshake response to {packet.sender_id_str}, payload size: {len(response)}")
904:             
905:             if self.encryption_service.is_session_established(packet.sender_id_str):
906:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake completed with {packet.sender_id_str}")
907:                 # Clear handshake attempt time on success (matching Swift)
908:                 self.handshake_attempt_times.pop(packet.sender_id_str, None)
909:                 peer_nickname = self.peers.get(packet.sender_id_str, Peer()).nickname or packet.sender_id_str
910:                 print(f"\r\033[K\033[92m✓ Secure session established with {peer_nickname}\033[0m")
911:                 print("> ", end='', flush=True)
912:                 # Add small delay before sending pending messages to avoid BLE congestion
913:                 await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
914:                 # Send any pending private messages
915:                 await self.send_pending_private_messages(packet.sender_id_str)
916:                 
917:         except Exception as e:
918:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake init failed with {packet.sender_id_str}: {e}")
919:             import traceback
920:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake error details: {traceback.format_exc()}")
921:             # Clear any partial handshake state
922:             self.encryption_service.clear_handshake_state(packet.sender_id_str)
923:     
924:     async def handle_noise_handshake_resp(self, packet: BitchatPacket):
925:         """Handle Noise handshake response"""
926:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Received handshake response from {packet.sender_id_str}")
927:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Recipient ID: {packet.recipient_id_str}, My ID: {self.my_peer_id}")
928:         
929:         # Check if this handshake response is for us
930:         if packet.recipient_id_str and packet.recipient_id_str != self.my_peer_id:
931:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake response not for us, ignoring")

934:         payload_size = len(packet.payload)

938:         try:
939:             # Convert bytearray to bytes for encryption service

941:             response = self.encryption_service.process_handshake_message(packet.sender_id_str, payload_bytes)
942:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] process_handshake_message returned: {bool(response)}, response size: {len(response) if response else 0}")

944:             if response:
945:                 # Send final handshake message
946:                 final_packet = create_bitchat_packet_with_recipient(
947:                     self.my_peer_id, packet.sender_id_str, MessageType.NOISE_HANDSHAKE_INIT, response, None  # Continue with same type
948:                 )
949:                 # Set TTL to 3 like iOS
950:                 final_data = bytearray(final_packet)
951:                 final_data[2] = 3
952:                 await self.send_packet(bytes(final_data))

954:             
955:             if self.encryption_service.is_session_established(packet.sender_id_str):
956:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake completed with {packet.sender_id_str}")
957:                 # Clear handshake attempt time on success (matching Swift)
958:                 self.handshake_attempt_times.pop(packet.sender_id_str, None)
959:                 peer_nickname = self.peers.get(packet.sender_id_str, Peer()).nickname or packet.sender_id_str
960:                 print(f"\r\033[K\033[92m✓ Secure session established with {peer_nickname}\033[0m")
961:                 print("> ", end='', flush=True)
962:                 # Add small delay before sending pending messages to avoid BLE congestion
963:                 await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
964:                 # Send any pending private messages
965:                 await self.send_pending_private_messages(packet.sender_id_str)
966:                 
967:         except Exception as e:
968:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake response failed with {packet.sender_id_str}: {e}")
969:             import traceback
970:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Handshake error details: {traceback.format_exc()}")
971:             # Clear any partial handshake state

974:     async def handle_noise_encrypted(self, packet: BitchatPacket, raw_data: bytes):
975:         """Handle Noise encrypted message"""
976:         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Received encrypted message from {packet.sender_id_str}")
977:         
978:         # Check if sender is blocked
979:         fingerprint = self.encryption_service.get_peer_fingerprint(packet.sender_id_str)
980:         if fingerprint and fingerprint in self.blocked_peers:
981:             debug_println(f"[BLOCKED] Ignoring encrypted message from blocked peer: {packet.sender_id_str}")
982:             return
983:         
984:         try:
985:             # Convert bytearray to bytes for encryption service
986:             payload_bytes = bytes(packet.payload) if isinstance(packet.payload, bytearray) else packet.payload
987:             
988:             # Decrypt the Noise encrypted payload using the improved method
989:             decrypted_payload = self.encryption_service.decrypt_from_peer(packet.sender_id_str, payload_bytes)
990:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Successfully decrypted {len(decrypted_payload)} bytes from {packet.sender_id_str}")
991:             
992:             # The decrypted payload should be a complete BitchatPacket (matching Swift implementation)
993:             # Swift creates: BitchatPacket(type: MessageType.message, ...) and encrypts the whole packet
994:             
995:             try:
996:                 # Check if the decrypted data starts with version 1 (BitchatPacket)
997:                 if len(decrypted_payload) > 0 and decrypted_payload[0] == 1:
998:                     # Parse the decrypted data as a complete BitchatPacket
999:                     inner_packet = parse_bitchat_packet(decrypted_payload)
1000:                     if inner_packet:
1001:                         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Decrypted inner packet: type={inner_packet.msg_type.name if hasattr(inner_packet.msg_type, 'name') else inner_packet.msg_type}, sender={inner_packet.sender_id_str}")
1002:                         
1003:                         # Verify this is a MESSAGE packet (as created by Swift)
1004:                         if inner_packet.msg_type == MessageType.MESSAGE:
1005:                             # Parse the message payload from the inner packet
1006:                             try:
1007:                                 message = parse_bitchat_message_payload(inner_packet.payload)
1008:                                 
1009:                                 # Check for duplicates
1010:                                 if message.id not in self.processed_messages:
1011:                                     self.bloom.add(message.id)
1012:                                     self.processed_messages.add(message.id)
1013:                                     
1014:                                     # Display the message as private
1015:                                     await self.display_message(message, packet, True)
1016:                                     
1017:                                     # Send ACK
1018:                                     await self.send_delivery_ack(message.id, packet.sender_id_str, True)
1019:                                 else:
1020:                                     debug_println(f"[DUPLICATE] Ignoring duplicate encrypted message: {message.id}")
